title: Remove Accent Color from H1-H5 Headers — Use Standard Text Colors
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want header text (H1-H5) to use standard dark/light text colors instead of the accent color, so that headers are not confused with clickable elements.
description:
The current styling guide applies the accent/primary color to heading tags (H1-H5). This creates confusion because the accent color is typically used for clickable elements. Headers should use standard text colors instead.

## Change
- Remove accent color from H1-H5 globally
- In dark mode: headers display in white
- In light mode: headers display in black/dark text
- Accent color reserved exclusively for interactive/clickable elements
acceptance_criteria: H1-H5 headers no longer use the accent/primary color
Headers display in white text in dark mode
Headers display in dark text in light mode
Accent color remains on clickable/interactive elements only
No visual regressions on pages that relied on colored headers
ux_changes: Removed accent/primary color from H1-H5 headers globally. Headers now use standard dark/light text colors. Accent color reserved for clickable elements only.
implementation_notes: Global CSS change removing the primary color from H1-H5 selectors. Dark mode uses white, light mode uses default dark text. This aligns with the design principle that accent color = clickable.
comment_summary: Developer removed accent color from H1-H5 and pushed to development. Dark mode headers use white, light mode headers use black.
